_note: Refreshed by the refresh-sports-feed scheduled task. Each item is {headline, url, source, published (ISO8601), snark}. Snark voice: Barstool-adjacent guy-at-the-bar, crass, horny, cocky, zero filler. One sentence. No slurs, no punching down at specific real people in a harmful way. Punch sideways (at media, front offices, rival fanbases) and up (at ownership). Keep it Boston.

items.headline: Red Sox vs Braves tonight: 18-24 Sox try to keep bats hot in Atlanta
items.snark: Sox are 17-23 and the offense still couldn't score in a batting cage with a pitching machine.

items.headline: Karon Prunty's Wake Forest senior surge landed him on Patriots draft board
items.snark: Late-round corner bet his future on one good year, and the Pats coordinator bought what he was selling.

items.headline: Bills avoid international game while Pats forced to schlep to Munich again
items.snark: Schedule makers love sending the Pats overseas while the Bills kick back in Orchard Park, tale as old as time.

items.headline: Patriots 2026 schedule: brutal start, five primetime games, Germany trip
items.snark: Pats open with three straight road games against playoff teams, because the schedule gods are sadists.

items.headline: Patriots face toughest opening stretch in 40 years per schedule analysts
items.snark: First team since 2019 Dolphins to open against three division winners, so congrats on the company.

items.headline: Patriots players shrug at schedule release: 17 games is 17 games, says Jared Wilson
items.snark: Players pretending they don't care about the schedule while fans dissect every bye week placement.

items.headline: Pats open Sept. 9 vs Seahawks in Super Bowl LX rematch on Wednesday night
items.snark: Drake Maye gets another crack at Seattle after they picked him twice and housed a fumble in February.

items.headline: Pats schedule release video shot at Six Flags: players on rollercoaster reveal games
items.snark: Christian Gonzalez and Marcus Jones rode a coaster to announce the slate, which is on brand for this roster.

items.headline: Patriots host Steelers Week 2 in home opener after mini bye from Wednesday kickoff
items.snark: Steelers beat the Pats 21-14 last year behind five turnovers, which is vintage Foxborough football.

items.headline: Patriots to face Lions in Munich Week 10, followed by Week 11 bye
items.snark: Pats return to Germany hoping for better results than the 2023 Mac Jones pick-fest in Frankfurt.
